diagrams.title: IPv6 Base Header Format
diagrams.description: Shows the 40-byte fixed IPv6 header with all 8 fields
diagrams.purpose: Understanding IPv6 packet structure and comparing with IPv4

diagrams.title: IPv6 Extension Header Chain
diagrams.description: Shows how multiple extension headers are chained using Next Header field
elements: Base Header → Hop-by-Hop Options → Routing → Fragment → Destination Options → TCP/UDP
Each header has Next Header field pointing to following header
Last header points to upper-layer protocol (6=TCP, 17=UDP)
diagrams.purpose: Understanding optional headers and packet processing order

diagrams.title: IPv6 Address Types and Scope
diagrams.description: Visual representation of IPv6 address categories
elements: Multicast: FF00::/8 with scope bits (FF02::1 = all nodes on link)
Anycast: Same format as unicast but assigned to multiple nodes
diagrams.purpose: Understanding IPv6 addressing architecture and address categories

diagrams.title: IPv6 Packet Fragmentation Process
diagrams.description: Shows source fragmentation mechanism in IPv6
elements: Fragment Header contains: Next Header, Fragment Offset (13 bits), M flag (More fragments)
diagrams.purpose: Understanding IPv6 fragmentation differs from IPv4
